summary.intent: Implement CHA-19: build a CRM tab in the moltis web UI by adding page-crm.js, locale files, and wiring Rust gon/nav-counts structs to expose the existing CRM backend via WebSocket RPC.
summary.outcome: Feature branch CHA-19-web-ui-crm-tab created and all 9 planned files implemented (4 new JS/locale files, 5 modified Rust/JS files); PR created and linked to Linear issue.

repo: CRM backend (types, SQLite store, RPC methods, migrations, PII protection) was 100% complete before this ticket — the entire web UI surface was missing.
All 14 CRM RPC methods already existed in crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs — no new Rust handlers needed for the UI.
The channels page (page-channels.js) is the canonical reference pattern for new feature pages: sendRpc() for all data access, signal() for state, single large file with internal Preact components.
Feature gating uses #[cfg(feature = 'crm')] in gateway; web side checks crm_enabled bool injected via GonData/gon.
SpaRoutes, NavCounts, and GonData structs in templates.rs are the extension points for adding new tabs — each field corresponds to a route path, badge count, or feature flag.
NavCounts uses tokio::join! for concurrent DB queries; adding CRM count means calling list_contacts().len() (full list load) — acceptable for v1.
PII fields (email, phone) are Secret<String> in Rust; LiveCrmService.contact_to_json() calls expose_secret() deliberately — authenticated API context makes this correct.
The eng review corrected the CEO review: CEO recommended ~15 new REST handlers, but RPC methods already existed. Eng review reduced scope from ~16 files to 9 files with zero new Rust handlers.

code.finding: SpaRoutes struct — add crm: &'static str field here for route injection into gon
NavCounts struct — add crm: usize field; build_nav_counts() uses tokio::join! for concurrent queries
All 14 CRM RPC methods registered here: crm.contacts.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.channels.list/upsert/delete, crm.matters.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.interactions.list/record/delete
CrmService trait + NoopCrmService default; GatewayServices.crm: Arc<dyn CrmService>
LiveCrmService — contact_to_json/matter_to_json/interaction_to_json serialize PII via expose_secret(); this is the shape of RPC responses the JS will receive

workflow: Multi-stage autonomous pipeline (ceo-review → eng-review → implement) with Linear as workpad works well; each stage appends to the same Linear comment thread.
ctx_batch_execute MCP tool failed with relative paths; always use absolute paths or the dedicated Read tool for file access in this workspace.
AskUserQuestion tool call was denied (headless mode) — the agent correctly fell back to making all decisions autonomously and documenting them for the human reviewer at the gate.

CEO review and eng review are separate skill invocations; eng review is where scope corrections happen — the CEO review's REST API recommendation was overridden by the eng review's deeper code analysis.
Writing the full review to a plans/ markdown file before posting to Linear is a useful pattern — it creates a local artifact and allows verification before API call.

friction: ctx_batch_execute MCP tool repeatedly failed to read files (relative path resolution issues), requiring fallback to direct Read tool calls — wasted several tool call rounds.
AskUserQuestion was denied mid-review (headless constraint), forcing the agent to document 5 unresolved architecture decisions and proceed with its own judgment.
The CEO review incorrectly recommended ~15 new REST handlers — this required the eng review to spend effort correcting scope rather than adding new value.
Linear API key was not available via env vars in the MCP context; required falling back to a raw shell-based curl/python call to post comments.
open_items: Optimistic locking for CRM updates — concurrent edits silently overwrite (last-write-wins); needs updated_at comparison on PUT. P2, Size M.
Matter kanban drag-and-drop pipeline view — deferred to Phase 2; v1 uses table with status dropdown.
Contact merge/dedup — detect and merge duplicate contacts across channels. P2, Size L.
E2E Playwright tests for CRM tab — test spec defined in eng review but implementation requires E2E infra changes; separate ticket.
Search LIKE wildcard escaping — searching for % or _ passes through unescaped, causing wider matches; P3, single-tenant only.
Contact count optimization — build_nav_counts loads all contacts just to count them; add SELECT COUNT(*) method when contact volume warrants it.
French and Chinese locale files (crm.js) created with English fallback strings — proper translations are a separate i18n pass.
PII reveal-on-click UI pattern — showing email/phone directly for v1; masking with reveal-on-click deferred as polish item.
